fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Vermont Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Vermont - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to vermont, respiratory therapist compact vermont, vermont respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Vermont Office of Professional Regulation — RT
board.mailing_address:
Office of Professional Regulation
Secretary of State
128 State Street
Montpelier, VT 05633-1101

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) Entry Level Examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Vermont.

steps.title: Create VOPLS Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the Vermont Online Professional Licensing System (VOPLS) at secure.vermont.gov/elicensing.

steps.title: Start Online Application
steps.description: Log in to VOPLS, select 'Apply for a New License' from the dropdown menu, and choose the Respiratory Care Practitioner application.

steps.title: Complete Application & Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form and pay the $100 initial application fee.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload or arrange for official transcripts and NBRC verification to be sent directly to the Office of Professional Regulation.

steps.title: Await OPR Review
steps.description: The Office of Professional Regulation will review your complete application and supporting materials. You can monitor your application status within VOPLS.

steps.title: Receive License
steps.description: Upon approval, your Vermont Respiratory Care Practitioner license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Vermont respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Vermont have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Vermont. To work in Vermont, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Vermont
hero.dek: To apply for an initial Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Vermont, use the Vermont Online Professional Licensing System (VOPLS). Endorsement applications require submission of a paper form.
